title: त्रिपुरसुन्दरी कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री त्रिपुरसुन्दरी कवचम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
बालार्कमण्डलाभासां चतुर्बाहुसमन्विताम्।
पाशाङ्कुशधनुर्बाणान् धारयन्तीं शिवां भजे॥

॥ कवचम् ॥
त्रिपुरसुन्दरी मे शिरः पातु ललाटे भुवनेश्वरी।
भ्रूमध्ये पातु मे नित्यं श्रीविद्या परदेवता॥ १॥

नेत्रयोः पातु मे देवी कामेश्वरी सदा शुभा।
कर्णयोः पातु मे नित्यं भगमालिनी शिवा॥ २॥

नासिकायां सदा पातु नित्यक्लिन्ना परात्परा।
ओष्ठयोः पातु मे देवी भेरुण्डा वज्रवैष्णवी॥ ३॥

जिह्वायां पातु मे नित्यं वह्निवासिनी शुभा।
चिबुके पातु मे देवी महावज्रेश्वरी सदा॥ ४॥

कण्ठे रक्षतु मे दुर्गा वक्षःस्थले च पार्वती।
हृदये पातु मे देवी त्रिपुरा परमेश्वरी॥ ५॥

नाभौ पातु महालक्ष्मीः कट्यां रक्षतु चण्डिका।
ऊरुयोः पातु मे गौरी जानुनोः शैलपुत्रिका॥ ६॥

जङ्घयोः पातु मे नित्यं कात्यायनी सुरेश्वरी।
पादयोः पातु मे देवी सिद्धविद्या सनातनी॥ ७॥

सर्वाङ्गे पातु मे नित्यं श्रीत्रिपुरसुन्दरी।
इदं कवचमज्ञात्वा यो जपेत् त्रिपुरां पराम्॥ ८॥

न तस्य जायते सिद्धिः कल्पकोटिशतैरपि।
कवचेन तु संयुक्तो मन्त्री सर्वार्थसाधकः॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री त्रिपुरसुन्दरी कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Tripura Sundari Kavacham ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Baalarkamandalaabhaasaam Chaturbaahusamanvitaam.
Paashaankushadhanurbaaanaan Dhaarayanteem Shivaam Bhaje.

|| Kavacham ||
Tripurasundari Me Shirah Paatu Lalaate Bhuvaneshvari.
Bhrumadhye Paatu Me Nityam Shrividya Paradevata. || 1 ||

Netrayoh Paatu Me Devi Kameshvari Sada Shubha.
Karnayoh Paatu Me Nityam Bhagamalini Shiva. || 2 ||

Naasikayaam Sada Paatu Nityaklinnaa Paraatparaa.
Oshthayoh Paatu Me Devi Bherundaa Vajravaishnavee. || 3 ||

Jihvaayaam Paatu Me Nityam Vahnivaasini Shubhaa.
Chibuke Paatu Me Devi Mahavajreshvari Sada. || 4 ||

Kanthe Rakshatu Me Durga Vakshahsthale Cha Parvati.
Hridaye Paatu Me Devi Tripuraa Parameshvari. || 5 ||

Naabhau Paatu Mahalakshmih Katyaam Rakshatu Chandikaa.
Uruyoh Paatu Me Gauri Jaanunoh Shailaputrikaa. || 6 ||

Janghayoh Paatu Me Nityam Kaatyaayani Sureshvari.
Paadayoh Paatu Me Devi Siddhavidyaa Sanaatani. || 7 ||

Sarvaange Paatu Me Nityam Shri Tripurasundari.
Idam Kavachamajnaatvaa Yo Japet Tripuraam Paraam. || 8 ||

Na Tasya Jaayate Siddhih Kalpakotishatairaapi.
Kavachena Tu Samyukto Mantri Sarvarthasaadhakah. || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Tripura Sundari Kavacham Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: यह कवच त्रिपुरसुन्दरी देवी से शरीर के प्रत्येक अंग की रक्षा हेतु प्रार्थना है। बाल सूर्य के समान कान्ति वाली, चतुर्भुजी, पाश-अंकुश-धनुष-बाण धारिणी देवी का ध्यान कर उनसे रक्षा प्रार्थना की जाती है। मस्तक पर त्रिपुरसुन्दरी, ललाट पर भुवनेश्वरी, भ्रूमध्य पर श्रीविद्या, नेत्रों पर कामेश्वरी, कानों पर भगमालिनी, नासिका पर नित्यक्लिन्ना, कण्ठ पर दुर्गा, हृदय पर त्रिपुरा, नाभि पर महालक्ष्मी, कटि पर चण्डिका तथा समस्त अंगों पर श्री त्रिपुरसुन्दरी रक्षा करती हैं। इस कवच के बिना मन्त्र जप फलदायी नहीं होता, किन्तु कवच से युक्त साधक सर्वार्थसिद्ध होता है।
benefits: Provides complete divine protection from all directions
Invokes success in shri vidya sadhana and tantric practices
Invokes the blessing of beauty, grace, and magnetic personality
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears and negative energies
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires and leads to spiritual liberation
description: The Tripura Sundari Kavacham, known in Sanskrit as त्रिपुरसुन्दरी कवचम्, is a divine armor prayer for protection in praise of Goddess Parvati. Rooted in Rudrayamala Tantra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to remove all fears and negative energies, grant success in Shri Vidya sadhana and tantric practices, and fulfill all desires and leads to spiritual liberation. This sacred text is ideally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Lalita Jayanti for enhanced blessings. At 9 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Tripura Sundari Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Tripura Sundari Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Divine armour of Goddess Tripura Sundari for protection and spiritual success.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Tripura Sundari Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Tripura Sundari Kavacham (त्रिपुरसुन्दरी कवचम्) is a classical Tantric protective-armor hymn dedicated to Goddess Lalita Tripura Sundari — the third of the ten Mahavidyas of the Shakta tradition and the central deity of the Sri Vidya sampradaya. Tripura Sundari is the supreme beauty of all three worlds (tri-pura sundari) — bhuh, bhuvah, svah — and the Para-shakti who pervades all reality. The kavacham is preserved in classical Tantric tradition (drawn from the Rudrayamala, Vamakeshwara Tantra and other Sri Vidya sources) and invokes various aspects of Lalita — Bala Tripura Sundari, Tripureshwari, Rajarajeshwari, Shodashi, Kameshwari — to protect every part of the devotee's body and grant the supreme phala of Sri Vidya bhakti and liberation. (Sources: Rudrayamala Tantra; Vamakeshwara Tantra; Sri Vidya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lalita Tripura Sundari and what is her place among the Mahavidyas?
seoFaqs.answer: Lalita Tripura Sundari is the third of the ten Mahavidyas (great wisdom-goddesses) and is universally regarded by the Sri Vidya sampradaya as the supreme Para-shakti — the absolute Brahman-Devi who manifests as the entire cosmos. The Lalita Sahasranama of the Brahmanda Purana describes her as Sri Mata, Sri Maharajni, Srimat Simhasaneshvari — the supreme mother seated on the lion-throne in her capital Sripura. Iconographically she is described as fair-complexioned and surpassingly beautiful (sundari), seated on the body of Shiva-Sadashiva, four-armed bearing pasha (noose), ankusha (goad), pushpa-bana (flower-arrows) and ikshu-danda (sugarcane bow), surrounded by her retinue of Mantrini, Dandanatha and other shaktis. She is the central deity of the Sri Chakra yantra and the Pancha Dashi (15-syllable) and Shodashi (16-syllable) mantras. (Sources: Lalita Sahasranama; Brahmanda Purana; Sri Vidya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is she called Tripura Sundari — the beauty of three cities?
seoFaqs.answer: The name Tripura Sundari means 'the surpassingly beautiful one of the three puras (cities or worlds).' Multiple traditional interpretations exist: (1) She is the most beautiful in the three lokas — bhuh (earth), bhuvah (atmosphere), svah (heaven); (2) She is supreme in the three states — jagrat (waking), svapna (dream), sushupti (deep sleep); (3) She is the Devi of the three deities — Brahma (srishti), Vishnu (sthiti), Rudra (samhara); (4) She presides over the three nadis — ida, pingala, sushumna; (5) She is supreme in the three Vedas — Rig, Yajur, Sama; (6) In Sri Vidya theology, she is the unifying Para-shakti underlying the three bindus of the Sri Chakra. The Lalita Sahasranama compiles dozens of names beginning with Tri- to celebrate this trifold supremacy. (Sources: Lalita Sahasranama Bhashya by Bhaskararaya; classical Sri Vidya commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Tripura Sundari, the Sri Chakra and the Sri Vidya mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Tripura Sundari, the Sri Chakra yantra and the Pancha Dashi / Shodashi mantra together form the inseparable triad — devata, yantra, mantra — at the heart of the Sri Vidya sampradaya. (1) Tripura Sundari is the Devi (deity) — the Para-shakti meditated upon as the supreme beauty seated on the bindu of the Sri Chakra; (2) The Sri Chakra (also called Sri Yantra) is her yantra (geometric form) — a complex mandala of nine interlocking triangles producing 43 smaller triangles arranged around a central bindu, surrounded by lotus petals and three protective walls; (3) The Pancha Dashi (15-syllable) mantra is her mantra — Ka-E-I-La-Hrim, Ha-Sa-Ka-Ha-La-Hrim, Sa-Ka-La-Hrim — which in its expanded form with the Shrim bija becomes the Shodashi (16-syllable) mantra. Initiation into the Sri Vidya mantra and yantra is the central diksha of the sampradaya, given by an authorised Sri Vidya guru. (Sources: Vamakeshwara Tantra; Yogini Hridaya; Sri Vidya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Tripura Sundari Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Tripura Sundari Kavacham is most powerful when recited on Fridays (Shukravar — sacred to all Devi forms and to Shukra-Lakshmi), Tuesdays (Mangalvar), every Pournima (full moon — supreme tithi of all Mahavidyas), and during the great Devi festivals: Lalita Panchami (Ashwin Shukla Panchami of Sharad Navaratri), Lalita Jayanti (Magha Purnima), Sri Vidya Navaratri, and Phalguna Purnima Holi-Lalita celebrations. Recitation should be at Brahma muhurta or after sunset, after a bath, facing east or north, on a red or pink asana, with offerings of red flowers (especially japa-kusuma — hibiscus), kumkum, akshata, panchamrita and red sweets. Tantric tradition strongly emphasizes that any serious Sri Vidya kavacha-paath should be undertaken under the guidance of an authorised Sri Vidya guru, ideally with prior diksha into at least the Bala Tripura Sundari mantra. (Sources: Rudrayamala Tantra; classical Sri Vidya paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Tripura Sundari Kavacham bestow according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Classical Sri Vidya tradition attributes the following benefits to regular recitation of the Tripura Sundari Kavacham: complete protection from all evil influences, black magic, drishti dosha and tantric attacks; pacification of the planets (Tripura Sundari being the navagraha-ishvari); cultivation of supreme beauty, charm and magnetism (saubhagya — the special blessing of Lalita); marital harmony and conjugal happiness; pacification of Mangal dosha and marriage obstacles; bestowal of vak-siddhi (mastery of speech), prosperity (sri), royal favor (raja-priti — Tripura Sundari being Rajarajeshwari, the queen of kings); cure of all chronic diseases through the grace of Lalita-Bhandasura-jit; cultivation of inner saumya (gentleness) and bliss; and the supreme phala of Sri Vidya — direct realisation of the non-dual Para-shakti as one's own innermost Self. (Sources: Lalita Sahasranama phalashruti; classical Sri Vidya commentaries.)
